title: Lesson Quiz: What Is Media Literacy?
description: Assessment of key concepts aligned to the lesson objective.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes this lesson concept?

choices.text: Media literacy is the ability to analyze, evaluate, and understand how media messages work
choices.feedback: Correct. This matches the core lesson idea.

choices.text: Technology use never requires judgment or reflection.
choices.feedback: This ignores the lesson focus on responsible decision-making.

choices.text: The best digital choice is always the fastest one.
choices.feedback: Speed does not guarantee responsibility or accuracy.

choices.text: Digital success depends only on technical skill.
choices.feedback: The lesson focuses on behavior, judgment, and responsible action, not only technical skill.

questions.prompt: Understanding how information is created and shared can improve decision-making.

choices.feedback: Correct. This statement aligns to the lesson objective.
This statement reflects the lesson concept and should be recognized as true.

questions.prompt: Which action best demonstrates the lesson skill in practice?

choices.text: Asking who created a message and why before accepting it as reliable
choices.feedback: Correct. That action applies the lesson concept responsibly.

choices.text: Acting immediately without reviewing the situation.
choices.feedback: This skips the lesson emphasis on thoughtful action.

choices.text: Ignoring possible consequences as long as the task feels easy.
choices.feedback: Responsible digital behavior requires attention to impact and consequences.

choices.text: Copying what others do instead of making an informed choice.
choices.feedback: The lesson emphasizes judgment, not imitation.

questions.prompt: Why is this lesson concept important?

choices.text: Media literacy improves judgment about credibility, purpose, and message design
choices.feedback: Correct. This explains why the concept matters in digital situations.

choices.text: It guarantees that digital risks can be eliminated completely.
choices.feedback: Responsible choices can reduce risk, but they do not eliminate all risk.

choices.text: It matters only when someone is being watched by a teacher or parent.
choices.feedback: The concept applies across digital life, not only under supervision.

choices.text: It replaces the need for responsibility, communication, or judgment.
choices.feedback: The lesson concepts support responsible thinking; they do not replace it.

questions.prompt: This lesson concept helps people make stronger digital decisions.

choices.feedback: Correct. The lesson skill supports safer, more informed, and more responsible digital choices.
The concept is intended to improve digital judgment and decision-making.
